# mdviewer Harness

**Generated by:** Mothra Harnesses
**Date:** 2026-07-24
**Location:** `C:\Develop\mdviewer\`
**Archetype:** custom

## What It Is

mdviewer is a zero-build, single-file Markdown reader that renders `.md` content with KaTeX formulas, highlight.js syntax highlighting, and Mermaid diagrams entirely client-side via CDN libraries. It ships as a standalone HTML file (browser-ready) and an optional Tauri v2 wrapper for desktop (Windows/macOS/Linux) and Android with native file dialogs, folder browsing via SAF/scoped-storage, file associations, and session persistence.

## 4 Primitives

| Primitive | Method | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| INIT | `mdviewer.html` open / `cargo tauri dev` | Load the single-page app in browser or Tauri webview; state initialised from `localStorage` |
| INJECT | Drag-and-drop / `fileInput.click()` / `window.__TAURI__.core.invoke('read_file')` | Feed a Markdown file, folder tree, or remote URL into the render pipeline |
| EXTRACT | `contentBody.innerHTML` / Rust command return value | Rendered HTML (with KaTeX, highlight.js, Mermaid) is written to `#content-body`; plain text or metadata available via DOM / IPC |
| KILL | Browser tab close / `process.exit()` / Tauri window close | No persistent background state; `saveSettings()` flushes to `localStorage` before unload |

## Installation

### Browser (no build)
No installation required. Double-click `mdviewer.html` in any modern browser. All libraries load from CDN on demand.

### Desktop (Tauri)
```bash
# Prerequisites: Rust 1.70+, WebView2 (Win), or libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev (Linux)
cd application/src-tauri
cargo tauri build
# Install the generated NSIS/MSI/DMG/AppImage
```

### Android
```bash
# Prerequisites: Rust 1.70+, Android Studio, SDK 34+, NDK 26+, Java 17+
cd application/src-tauri
cargo tauri android build --apk
# Install the APK from gen/android/app/build/outputs/apk/
```

## Usage

### Browser (direct)

Open `mdviewer.html` in any browser. All features work without a server:

- **Open file**: Click toolbar "打开" or press `Ctrl+O` → native file picker → FileReader reads the `.md` file → `render(markdown, name)` parses and displays
- **Open folder**: Click "文件夹" or drag a folder onto the window → `readDirRecursive()` scans with `webkitGetAsEntry()` → sidebar file tree → click any `.md` to load
- **URL import**: Click "URL" → enter a raw Markdown URL → `fetch()` retrieves and renders
- **Drop files/folders**: Drag any file or folder onto the dropzone → auto-detects single file vs directory

### From agents (programmatic)

Agents can invoke mdviewer's rendering pipeline directly from Node.js or browser context:

```javascript
// The core render pipeline (works in any JS context)
const { text, blocks } = protectMath(markdownSource);
let html = marked.parse(text);
html = html.replace(/\x00M_(\d+)\x00/g, (_, idx) => {
  const b = blocks[+idx];
  if (!b) return '';
  return katex.renderToString(b.e, { displayMode: !!b.d, throwOnError: false });
});
// Result: fully rendered HTML with LaTeX, syntax-highlighted code, and Mermaid placeholders
```

### Tauri IPC commands

When running inside the Tauri webview, agents can invoke Rust commands:

```javascript
await window.__TAURI__.core.invoke('read_file', { path: '/path/to/file.md' });
// Returns: { content: "markdown string", name: "file.md", path: "/path/to/file.md" }

await window.__TAURI__.core.invoke('scan_folder', { path: '/path/to/folder' });
// Returns: [{ name: "file.md", path: "/path/to/folder/file.md" }, ...]
```

### Tauri scoped-storage commands (Android only)

```javascript
const result = await window.__TAURI__.core.invoke('plugin:scoped-storage|pick_folder', {});
// result.folder = { id: "...", name: "...", uri: "..." }

const entries = await window.__TAURI__.core.invoke('plugin:scoped-storage|read_dir', { folderId: "..." });
// entries.entries = [{ name: "...", path: "...", is_dir: false, ... }]

const file = await window.__TAURI__.core.invoke('plugin:scoped-storage|read_text_file', { folderId: "...", fileName: "..." });
// file.contents = "markdown string"
```

### CLI

No CLI is built for the browser version. The Tauri desktop app has no CLI arguments; all interactions are GUI-based.

## Settings persistence

All settings are stored in `localStorage` under key `mdviewer-settings` as a JSON blob:

```javascript
const state = { settings: loadSettings() };
// Defaults are applied at read-time via || fallbacks in applySettings() / syncSettingUI()
```

| Setting key | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `theme` | string | `light` | One of: light, dark, sepia, nord, gruvbox, monokai, solarized, solarized-dark, custom |
| `customColors` | object | — | RGB hex overrides for bg/text/heading/link/codeBg/border |
| `codeTheme` | string | `auto` | auto, light, dark |
| `fontFamily` | string | `sans` | sans, serif, mono |
| `contentWidth` | string | `medium` | narrow, medium, wide, full |
| `fontSize` | number | 16 | Document body font size (px, 12–48) |
| `contentFontSize` | number | 100 | Content area zoom percentage (50–300) |
| `lineHeight` | number | 1.75 | Content line height (1.3–2.2) |
| `sidebarFontSize` | number | 13 | Sidebar font size (px, 10–24) |
| `alwaysShowToolbar` | bool | false | Pin toolbar visible on scroll |
| `swapSidebarTabs` | bool | false | Swap files/toc tab order |
| `swipeSidebar` | bool | true | Enable swipe gesture for sidebar (mobile) |
| `saveSession` | bool | true | Persist last opened file/folder to localStorage |
| `autoDarkMode` | bool | false | Follow system color scheme (light/dark only) |
| `shortcuts` | object | — | Custom key bindings per action |

Session data is stored under key `mdviewer-session`:

```javascript
{
  type: "tauri" | "scoped" | "url",
  name: "file.md",
  path: "/full/path/to/file.md",           // type=tauri
  folderId: "...",                         // type=scoped
  folderName: "folder-name",               // type=scoped
  activeRelPath: "subdir/file.md",         // type=scoped
  url: "https://...",                      // type=url
  sidebarVisible: true,
  activeTab: "files" | "toc"
}
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `TAURI_DEV_SERVER_PORT` | no | 1420 | Dev server port for Tauri hot-reload (development only) |
| `ANDROID_SDK_ROOT` | yes (Android) | — | Path to Android SDK (e.g., `C:\Users\...\AppData\Local\Android\Sdk`) |
| `NDK` | yes (Android) | — | Path to Android NDK |

## Dependencies

- **Browser**: None. All libraries load from CDN: marked 15.0.12, KaTeX 0.16.11, highlight.js 11.9.0, Mermaid 11.16.0
- **Desktop (Tauri)**: Rust edition 2021, serde/serde_json 1, walkdir 2, tauri 2, tauri-plugin-dialog 2, tauri-plugin-opener 2
- **Android**: tauri-plugin-scoped-storage 1 (in addition to desktop deps)
- No npm, no bundler, no build step for the browser version

## Key Architecture

### Render pipeline (order matters)

```
protectMath() → marked.parse() → replace \x00M_N\x00 placeholders with KaTeX
  → highlight.js on <pre><code> blocks → Mermaid render on .language-mermaid
  → addHeadingAnchors() → rebuildToc()
```

### State flow

```
state.settings ←→ localStorage (loadSettings / saveSettings)
       ↓
applySettings() → sets CSS custom properties on document.documentElement
       → syncSettingUI() → UI controls reflect current state
       → applyZoomBtn() → floating zoom button shows percentage
```

### Tauri branching

All platform-specific code follows this pattern:

```javascript
if (window.__TAURI__) {
  // Desktop: native dialog / IPC
  const data = await window.__TAURI__.core.invoke('read_file', { path });
} else if (isAndroid) {
  // Android: scoped-storage plugin
  const result = await window.__TAURI__.core.invoke('plugin:scoped-storage|read_dir', { folderId });
} else {
  // Browser: FileReader / fetch
  const text = await fetch(url).then(r => r.text());
}
```

### CSS theming

Nine themes via `[data-theme="light"|dark|sepia|nord|gruvbox|monokai|solarized|solarized-dark|custom]` on `<html>`. Custom colors are applied via `element.style.setProperty()` overrides. All visual styling uses CSS custom properties — no hardcoded colors outside `:root` / theme blocks.

## Mobile (Android) Features

- **Folder browsing**: Uses `tauri-plugin-scoped-storage` with Android SAF `ACTION_OPEN_DOCUMENT_TREE`. Handles persist across restarts via `list_folders`.
- **File opening**: Falls back to browser `FileReader` when Tauri dialog is unavailable on Android.
- **Swipe sidebar**: Touch gesture — right-swipe from left edge to open, left-swipe on sidebar or swipe on overlay to close. Controlled by `state.settings.swipeSidebar`.
- **Pinch zoom**: Two-finger pinch on `#content-inner` adjusts `contentFontSize` (50–300%). Double-tap resets to 100%. A floating button at bottom-right shows current percentage.
- **Mobile sheet**: "···" toolbar button opens a bottom sheet (`#mobileSheet`) with file open, folder open, URL, sidebar toggle, options, and close buttons.
- **Safety area**: CSS `env(safe-area-inset-*)` variables for notched/rounded displays.
- **Session restore toast**: `showToast(msg, 0)` shows a persistent "Restoring session..." message; `dismissToast(el)` removes it after async recovery completes.

## Notes & Quirks

- **Single-file constraint**: `mdviewer.html` contains all CSS, HTML, and JS inline. The Service Worker (`sw.js`) and manifest (`manifest.json`) must remain separate files per browser spec.
- **Frontend must be synced**: `application/frontend/index.html` is an exact copy of `mdviewer.html`. Use `Copy-Item` after every edit. Build scripts (`build.ps1` / `build.bat`) do this automatically.
- **Android scoped-storage response wrapping**: Kotlin-side commands wrap responses differently from Rust-side commands. The JS adapter layer handles both — e.g., `folder.folder || folder` for `pick_folder`, `handles.folders` for `list_folders`, `entries.entries` for `read_dir`.
- **Zoom scroll preservation**: `setContentFontSize()` remembers the element at the viewport top via `elementFromPoint()` before changing font size, then applies scroll delta via `requestAnimationFrame` to prevent viewport jumping.
- **Auto dark mode**: Only active when the selected theme is `light` or `dark`. Selecting any other theme (sepia, nord, monokai, custom, etc.) disables auto-follow of the system color scheme.
- **Save session toggle**: When disabled, `saveSession()` returns immediately without writing to localStorage. Session restore is skipped on next launch.
- **Keyboard shortcuts**: 8 default shortcuts are configurable via the settings panel. Shortcut state is stored in `state.settings.shortcuts` and rendered by `renderShortcutList()`. Capturing mode (`capturingShortcut`) blocks normal input until a key is pressed.

---

*Forged by Mothra Harnesses v1.0.0*
